rulings.Case Title: MARY C. MCFADDEN VS. MARY L. MCFADDEN, AS TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Request For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 2, 2021 line 1. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES is continued to January 4, 2022 for parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of their respective filings no later than December 23, 2021 with cover letters reflecting new hearing date. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNY SAI-YUN SHU CHANG ET AL VS. SARA SHU CHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defendants Motion To Enforce Terms Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 2, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFFS JENNY SHU CHANG, STELLA SHU LOO AND PHILLIP SHU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR DEFENDANTS MOTION TO ENFORCE TERMS OF SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is continued to February 3, 2022. Plaintiff to initiate arbitration regarding disputed costs. No later than December 24, 2021 Plaintiff to file a declaration regarding the status of arbitration including the date of the arbitration and the anticipated date of the decision (to be obtained form the arbitrator). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE S. DORFMAN VS. RICHARD CARTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Plaintiff?S Motion And Motion To Appoint Partition Referee Per Ccp 873.010
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 2, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE DORFMAN'S RE-NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPOINT PARTITION REFEREE PER CCP 873.010 is continued to December 23, 2021. No later than December 17, 2021 parties to submit (1) a joint brief identifying an agreed-upon referee; or (2) submit separate briefs identifying two proposed referees each. No further briefing is allowed. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BUSH STREET APARTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. 1010 BUSH RESIDENCE, LLC ET AL
